rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe but non-systemic lichen planus flare — needs prompt prescription and baseline bloods with dermatology follow-up, and a clear safety-net for erosive oral disease and drug-eruption/SCAR. Not an emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Mouth patches becoming painful open ulcers preventing eating/drinking
escalation_edges.rationale: Erosive oral LP can impair intake and needs specialist management.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the white patches in your mouth turn into painful open ulcers that stop you eating or drinking, get reviewed promptly.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Widespread blistering rash or skin peeling
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests a severe cutaneous adverse reaction (SJS/TEN-type).
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a widespread blistering rash or your skin starts peeling — go to the emergency department urgently.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear written instructions and safety-net thresholds; ensure follow-up for bloods and chronic care.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is lichen planus. Use the strong steroid ointment on the bumps, take the short steroid course and a night antihistamine to sleep, don't scratch, and get the blood test done. Watch for painful mouth ulcers or a widespread blistering rash.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within days for prescription/bloods; urgent review/ED for erosive-oral or blistering features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for the prescription; ED for widespread blistering/skin peeling.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document cutaneous + oral LP flare, treatment started, HCV serology ordered, and dermatology follow-up.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: OTC-cream self-care only with no drug/SCAR screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A lichenoid drug eruption/SCAR is missed and progresses, or erosive oral disease impairs intake unnoticed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling without adequate treatment or HCV/oral follow-up.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED referral for an uncomplicated LP flare without SCAR/erosive-oral features.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot dermoscope/biopsy or fully examine the mouth remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as LP with potent topical ± short oral steroid, arrange bloods incl. HCV and dermatology follow-up, and safety-net for SCAR/erosive-oral disease.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: OTC-cream advice only, or missing a drug eruption/SCAR.
